meanings.definition: exercise authoritative control or power over
lessen the intensity of; temper; hold in restraint; hold or keep within limits

examples: do not operate machinery after imbibing alcohol

meanings.definition: control (others or oneself) or influence skillfully, usually to one's advantage
examples: She is a very controlling mother and doesn't let her children grow up
The teacher knew how to keep the class in line

meanings.definition: check or regulate (a scientific experiment) by conducting a parallel experiment or comparing with another standard
examples: Are you controlling for the temperature?

meanings.definition: verify by using a duplicate register for comparison
be careful or certain to do something; make certain of something
examples: He verified that the valves were closed
See that the curtains are closed
control the quality of the product

meanings.definition: have a firm understanding or knowledge of; be on top of
able to control or determine policy
examples: a controlling interest in the firm
